title: Mutton Karahi

seo.title: How to Make Mutton Karahi — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Mutton Karahi. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Mutton Karahi — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Mutton Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish, known for its rich, spicy tomato-ginger base and tender mutton pieces. This traditional stir-fried curry is cooked in a karahi (wok), allowing the flavors to meld beautifully, creating an aromatic and deeply satisfying meal. Perfect for family dinners or special occasions, it promises an authentic taste of Pakistani cuisine.

instructions.title: Brown the Mutton
instructions.text: Heat cooking oil in a large karahi or heavy-bottomed pot over high heat. Add the mutton pieces and sear them until lightly browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. This locks in the juices and adds initial flavor. Drain any excess water that might accumulate.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Spices
instructions.text: Reduce heat to medium. Add ginger-garlic paste to the mutton and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant. Stir in salt, red chili powder, turmeric powder, roasted cumin powder, and roasted coriander powder. Cook for another 1 minute, stirring constantly to ensure spices coat the mutton well.

instructions.title: Cook Down Tomatoes
instructions.text: Add the diced or pureed tomatoes to the karahi. Cover and cook on medium-low heat for about 8 minutes, or until the tomatoes have softened completely and the oil begins to separate. Mash the tomatoes with the back of your spoon to create a smooth gravy base.

instructions.title: Simmer for Tenderness
instructions.text: Pour in about 1/2 cup of water. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the mutton is tender. Check periodically and add a splash more water if needed to prevent drying. Ensure the mutton is cooked through.

instructions.title: Finish with Flavor Enhancers
instructions.text: Once the mutton is tender and the gravy has reached your desired consistency, stir in the whisked yogurt (if using), slit green chilies, black pepper, and garam masala powder. Cook for another 3 minutes without the lid, stirring occasionally, until the oil completely separates and the gravy thickens beautifully. Adjust seasoning as needed.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish generously with fresh chopped coriander and julienned ginger. Serve the authentic Mutton Karahi immediately and hot with freshly baked naan, roti, or fluffy plain rice for a complete and satisfying Pakistani meal.

tips: For an authentic Karahi taste, use a traditional wok-like karahi. The wide surface area allows for quick evaporation and intense flavor development.
Always use fresh, ripe tomatoes for the best flavor; avoid canned tomatoes if possible for the true Karahi essence. If you prefer a richer gravy, a touch of cream can be added at the end.
Mutton Karahi tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld further. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop.
article: Mutton Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
